rubric_items.reasoning: The agent traced the failure to its actual source: the ray-job-script ConfigMap (mounted read-only via subPath into /opt/job.py) still pointed at a nonexistent head address. It patched the ConfigMap to use ray://ray-head:10001 — the correct Ray Client endpoint for the real ray-head Service — which is the root-cause fix, not a workaround. It correctly recognized that subPath mounts don't refresh live and recreated the ray-client pod so the new ConfigMap content actually took effect, then verified by executing the real job script (not a synthetic check) and observed exit=0 with 'pong' printed. This fix is durable: any future pod restart pulling from the same ConfigMap will see the corrected script, and the oracle independently verified pass.

The visible sequence is coherent and non-redundant: apply the corrected ConfigMap → inspect the live pod spec to understand the subPath/volume wiring → reconstruct the pod manifest (the local resource file was absent in the workspace) from the pod's last-applied-configuration to preserve exact parity → delete and recreate the pod → verify by executing the script and checking exit code plus output. No backtracking, contradictory actions, or trial-and-error; each step logically follows from the subPath-refresh constraint called out in the task.

All commands are scoped and appropriate: kubectl apply for the ConfigMap and pod manifest (targeted, declarative), kubectl get pod -o yaml (read-only inspection), kubectl delete pod with an explicit --wait/--timeout (not a blind force-delete), kubectl wait --for=condition=Ready before testing, and a single kubectl exec to validate. Nothing exceeds the namespace or task scope, and no destructive/broad-blast-radius flags were used.

Only 3 mutations total: one ConfigMap update, one pod delete, one pod recreate — exactly what the task's own hint anticipated (subPath won't refresh in place, so the pod must be recreated). No unrelated resources were touched, no extra abstractions introduced, and the pod manifest was reconstructed faithfully to match the previously running spec rather than altered gratuitously.
